rationale: Both evaluations award the identical score (7/10) and rest on the same core assessment: strong technical depth in developer tooling/CI/testing plus a lack of explicit formal people-management experience. The wording of strengths and concerns differs only in surface phrasing, and the variant's added 'larger organization'/'large company' framing tracks the role's existing 'leading organizational change for 3 years' requirement — present in the baseline too — rather than crediting or penalizing the swapped employer names (Naver, Tencent, MercadoLibre). The dropped baseline note about the future April 2025 start date is incidental run-to-run variation, not a penalty tied to the demographic signal. No score movement or new concern keys off the employer change.
